items.title: Teaching Assistant — Info 2850: Networks
items.description: Led weekly lab sections for 60 undergraduates; designed problem sets covering graph theory, diffusion models, and network centrality. Held two office hours sessions per week.

items.title: Undergraduate Research Mentor
items.description: Mentoring two junior undergraduates on independent research projects related to information credibility and platform design on social media.

items.title: Peer Reviewer
items.description: Peer reviewer for top venues in human-computer interaction, computational social science, and web science. Completed 15+ reviews to date.

items.title: Graduate Student Representative
items.description: Elected representative for Ph.D. student concerns. Organized the department's annual research symposium with 120+ attendees and co-authored the student wellness survey.

items.title: Invited Talk: "Designing for Trust"
items.organization: MIT Media Lab, Human Dynamics Group
items.description: Presented work on how platform design choices interact with user trust in the context of algorithmic content curation. Q&A with faculty and graduate researchers.

items.title: Podcast Interview
items.description: Discussed the corrections backfire research and its implications for platform content moderation policy, in conversation with host Maya Chen.

items.title: InfoTrack
items.description: An open-source Python toolkit for tracking cross-platform information spread. Supports data collection from four platforms via their APIs. 200+ GitHub stars, actively maintained.

items.title: SurveyFlow
items.description: A lightweight library for designing and deploying survey experiments on Prolific and MTurk with attention checks, manipulation checks, and automated exclusion criteria.
